source.rulebook_title: ASCE/AISC Student Steel Bridge Competition 2022 Rules
source.rulebook_used: 2022 (publicly accessible). Structural rules -- stringer geometry, member box, two-decking load test, aggregate deflection scoring, lateral tests -- are stable year-over-year through 2025/2026 per SSBC clarifications page.
source.citations_anchor: Every numeric value in this sample is traceable to an SSBC 2022 rule subsection; see `rulebook_citations` at the bottom of this file.

full_prompt:
Design a scale-model bolted steel bridge that satisfies the ASCE/AISC Student Steel Bridge Competition rules. The bridge must span a full-scale ~20 ft opening at the scale defined by the competition site and must be assembled from many short members joined by loose bolts under timed construction, not as a monolithic structure. The bridge has a west end (backspan, supported) and an east end (cantilevered). The deliverable is a full assembly consisting of three part families: (A) longitudinal chord/stringer-forming members, (B) lateral and diagonal web members that brace the two stringer lines, and (C) loose bolts used to connect members at their faying surfaces. Per SSBC rule 9.2.1 the bridge shall have exactly two stringers, each of which is contiguous; per rule 9.3.6 each stringer shall be at minimum 20 feet (6096 mm) long and at maximum 21 feet (6401 mm) long. Per rule 9.3.7 the bridge shall not be wider than 3 ft 7 in (1092 mm) at any location along the span. Per rule 8.2.2.2 every fabricated member (including the members that together form each stringer) shall fit inside a right rectangular prism with inner dimensions 3 ft 6 in by 6 in by 4 in (1067 mm x 152 mm x 102 mm); this makes the bridge intrinsically multi-part because no single stringer can be a single member. Members within a single fabricated piece may be welded together (rule 8.2.2), but members shall be connected to each other in the constructed bridge only with loose bolts in loose or welded nuts (rule 10.1 and 9.4.1 -- cam locks, dovetails, sleeved or interlocking connections that resist movement without a bolt are prohibited). Loose bolts shall have nominal length not exceeding 3 in (76.2 mm) measured from the bottom of the head to the end, shall be commercially available, and shall have external threads around the full circumference (rule 8.2.3). Loose nuts shall have the external shape of a hexagonal prism and have matching internal threads (rule 8.2.4). All members, loose bolts, and loose nuts shall be ferromagnetic (rule 10.4.2 inspection); steel grade is at the team's discretion.

Load tests (rule 11). The bridge undergoes two lateral load tests and one vertical load test. For the vertical test two grating decking units, each 3 ft (914 mm) long in the span direction, are placed on top of both stringers (rule 11.5.1.1). The first decking unit is placed at longitudinal position L on the backspan (L is determined by die roll immediately before the first bridge of the day is tested, rule 9 competition sequence step 8, and applies to every team in the same regional). The second decking unit is placed 1 in (25.4 mm) from the east end of the bridge measured along the top of the south side stringer (rule 11.5.1.1). Vertical loading sequence (rule 11.5.3): (i) 100 lb (445 N) preload is distributed on the backspan decking unit at L, and 50 lb (222 N) preload is distributed on the cantilever decking unit. (ii) The deflection measurement devices at D1 (on the backspan) and D2 (on the cantilever) are zeroed. (iii) 1600 lb (7117 N) additional live load is placed on the backspan decking unit. (iv) 750 lb (3336 N) additional live load is placed on the cantilever decking unit. The total applied weight (preload + live) is therefore 2500 lb (11 124 N). Lateral load tests (rule 11.4): a 50 lb (222 N) lateral load is applied to the north side of the bridge at the level of the top of the stringer, centered on a decking unit positioned 6 ft (1829 mm) from the west end (backspan sway test); a second 50 lb lateral load is applied at the top of the south side stringer 1 in from the east end (cantilever sway test). During the lateral tests approximately 75 lb (334 N) of dead weight is placed on the opposite-side decking to prevent lift-off (rule 11.4).

Pass/fail criteria and scoring (rule 6). Aggregate deflection is defined as the sum of the absolute values of the vertical deflections measured at D1 and D2 (rule 3, definition `Aggregate deflection`). (R1) For the bridge to avoid the structural-efficiency penalty, aggregate deflection under the full 2500 lb load shall be less than 2 in (50.8 mm); per rule 11.5.3, if aggregate deflection exceeds 2 in the scoring spreadsheet adds $4,000,000 to construction cost Cc and $10,000,000 to structural efficiency cost Cs. (R2) If at any point during loading any measured deflection exceeds 2.5 in (63.5 mm) upward or downward, judges halt the test and load is removed (rule 11.2.3.9 and 11.5.3 stop condition); the bridge is then disqualified from structural scoring. (R3) Total weight (measured weight plus dimensional-violation weight penalties) is the primary lightness score; the Cs step function penalizes measured weight above 150 lb (68.0 kg) and again above 300 lb (136.1 kg), so a design target for a competitive bridge is measured weight below 150 lb (rule 6 Structural Cost formulas). (R4) Dimensional conformance: every member must fit inside the 3'-6 in x 6 in x 4 in box; the bridge shall not be wider than 3 ft 7 in at any location; each stringer shall be 20-21 ft long. Dimensional violations add weight penalties of 20, 100, 200, or 400 lb depending on magnitude (rule 9.3.1.1). (R5) Lateral sway during the lateral tests is measured but there is no hard numeric sway limit in rule 11.4; sway is recorded and aggregated into the data form.

Verification. The primary verification is linear-static FEA of the full assembly with discrete-fastener representation of every loose-bolt connection. Four independent load cases shall be analyzed. Load Case V-B (vertical backspan): 1600 lb live plus 100 lb preload on a 3 ft decking unit at position L (which shall be taken at the most adverse position permitted by the backspan geometry -- the analyst shall report results for the worst-case L). Load Case V-C (vertical cantilever): 750 lb live plus 50 lb preload on a 3 ft decking unit placed 1 in from the east end; to be run simultaneously with V-B for the aggregate deflection check. Load Case L-B (lateral backspan): 50 lb lateral on the north side of a decking unit positioned 6 ft from the west end, with 75 lb dead weight on the opposite side. Load Case L-C (lateral cantilever): 50 lb lateral at the east end. A linear buckling eigenvalue analysis shall be run under the combined V-B + V-C load state; the first-mode load factor shall be >= 2.0 (this is a designer-imposed factor to guard against unexpected snap-through during loading, not a rulebook requirement). Vibration analysis, thermal analysis, fluid analysis, and radiation analysis are explicitly not required: the competition is judged statically, indoors, on a prepared floor. The bridge shall be modeled with beam or shell elements that respect the prismatic cross-sections the team chooses; loose-bolt connections shall be modeled as tied or coupled pairs with stiffness equivalent to the selected bolt in single or double shear, such that the assembly-level deflection prediction can be directly compared with measured D1 and D2.

Deliverables. (a) A STEP AP242 file containing every fabricated member and every loose-bolt instance as distinct solid bodies, with metadata identifying each body's part family (A, B, or C) and instance index. (b) A bill of materials counting each unique member cross-section and length, each unique bolt size, and each unique nut size. (c) An FEA report listing (i) aggregate deflection prediction under V-B + V-C, (ii) measured weight prediction in pounds and kilograms, (iii) max von Mises stress per member family per load case, (iv) first buckling load factor under V-B + V-C, (v) worst-case connection demand/capacity ratio assuming a nominal commercial bolt (the team shall state the assumed bolt grade), and (vi) predicted lateral sway under L-B and L-C. (d) A rule-compliance checklist citing rule numbers 8.2.2.2, 8.2.3.2, 8.2.4, 9.2.1, 9.3.6, 9.3.7, 10.1, and 11 point-by-point.
short_prompt:
Design a scale-model bolted steel bridge per the ASCE/AISC Student Steel Bridge Competition 2022 rules, delivered as a multi-part assembly of short bolted members (SSBC rule 8.2.2.2 forces every fabricated member inside a 3 ft 6 in x 6 in x 4 in box, so a 20 ft stringer cannot be a single piece). The bridge has exactly two contiguous stringers (rule 9.2.1), each 20-21 ft long (rule 9.3.6), width <= 3 ft 7 in (rule 9.3.7), with a west-end backspan and east-end cantilever joined only by loose bolts (<= 3 in length per rule 8.2.3).

Load tests per rule 11.
- V-B vertical backspan: 100 lb (445 N) preload + 1600 lb (7117 N) live on a 3 ft decking unit at position L.
- V-C vertical cantilever: 50 lb (222 N) preload + 750 lb (3336 N) live on a 3 ft decking unit 1 in from the east end.
- L-B lateral backspan sway: 50 lb (222 N) at 6 ft from the west end, with ~75 lb (334 N) opposite-side dead weight.
- L-C lateral cantilever sway: 50 lb (222 N) at the east end, with ~75 lb opposite-side dead weight.

Aggregate deflection = |D1| + |D2| under V-B + V-C must be < 2 in (50.8 mm) to avoid the $4,000,000 Cc + $10,000,000 Cs penalty (rule 11.5.3); any measured deflection >= 2.5 in (63.5 mm) halts the test. Total measured weight targets < 150 lb (68.0 kg) to stay in the lowest Cs bracket. A designer-imposed linear-buckling factor >= 2.0 is required under combined V-B + V-C.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Aggregate deflection = |D1| + |D2| is the scored competition metric; computed by summing absolute values of the two measured deflections per rule definition, not by any FEA-internal norm.

Loose-bolt shear/bearing capacity is a code-check calculation on top of FEA member forces. Since the rules permit any commercial bolt, the team selects the bolt and reports its capacity using AISC manual tables.
examples: AISC Steel Construction Manual Part 7 tables

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Rule 8.2.2.2 (3'-6 x 6 x 4 member box), rule 9.3.6 (20-21 ft stringer), rule 9.3.7 (width <= 3'-7) are geometric pass/fail checks performed by rigid templates during judging; reproduced in software as bounding-box containment on each member.

prompt.title: Two-stringer cantilever steel bridge (ASCE SSBC 2022 Rules)
prompt.brief: Bolted multi-member steel bridge assembly per SSBC 2022 rulebook. Exactly 2 contiguous stringers (20-21 ft), west-end backspan + east-end cantilever, every member <= 3'-6 x 6 x 4 inches, vertical load 1600 lb backspan + 750 lb cantilever, aggregate deflection scored (hard penalty >2 in), plus two 50 lb lateral sway tests.

parts.name: chord_stringer_member
parts.role: a single fabricated piece that, when connected in sequence with other A-family pieces, forms one of the two contiguous stringers along the span
parts.count_note: At least 12: two stringers, each stringer is at least 6 ft 96 mm long, each member is at most 3 ft 6 in long, so each stringer requires at least ceil(20 ft / 3.5 ft) = 6 members. Teams typically use more (shorter) pieces to fit truss-like configurations.

geometric_constraints.bounding_box_note: Inner dimensions of the inspection box (SSBC rule 8.2.2.2). Every fabricated member, regardless of part family, must fit inside this box.
geometric_constraints.cross_section: team-selected; prismatic; compatible with bolt-only connections at faying surfaces
geometric_constraints.contiguity_requirement: per SSBC rule 9.2.1 the chain of A-members shall form exactly two contiguous stringers with no vertical offset exceeding 1/4 in and no horizontal separation exceeding 1/4 in between adjacent members (rule 9.3.9)
prohibited: welded joints between separate members (welds within a single fabricated piece are allowed)

parts.name: lateral_and_diagonal_member
parts.role: lateral braces, diagonals, and cross-ties that connect the two stringer lines and resist vertical bending, lateral sway, and torsion
parts.count_note: Minimum count is architecture-dependent; a simple parallel-chord truss needs at least one diagonal per panel.

prohibited: see part family A prohibited list

parts.name: loose_bolt_and_nut
parts.role: fastens adjacent members at faying surfaces; the only permitted connection mechanism between two separate members

geometric_constraints.bolt_thread_requirement: external threads around full circumference of the shank (SSBC rule 8.2.3.2)
geometric_constraints.nut_shape_requirement: external shape of a hexagonal prism over full length with matching internal threads (SSBC rule 8.2.4)
geometric_constraints.bolt_diameter: team-selected; must match the hole pattern in each member (SSBC rule 9.4.3: hole must be completely surrounded by the member, and the outer-ply hole must be small enough that the nut or bolt head cannot pass through)
prohibited: threaded rod welded to the bridge (rule 8.2.3)
nuts or bolts that flex or move

assembly_envelope.west_end: non-cantilevered (backspan); both stringers land on footings here

material.name: any ferromagnetic steel, team-selected
material.rule_constraint: SSBC rule 10.4.2: magnetic inspection is part of timed construction. Non-magnetic members, nuts, or loose bolts incur weight penalties (25 lb per non-compliant nut or loose bolt, 35 lb per non-compliant member).

load_cases.name: vertical_backspan
load_cases.distribution: distributed across a 3 ft (914 mm) long grating decking unit placed on top of both stringers at longitudinal position L on the backspan
load_cases.notes: Position L is determined by die roll at the start of the competition day and is the same for every team; analyst shall assume the adversely worst L permitted by backspan geometry.

load_cases.name: vertical_cantilever
load_cases.distribution: distributed across a 3 ft (914 mm) long grating decking unit placed 1 in (25.4 mm) from the east end, centered on both stringers

load_cases.name: lateral_backspan_sway
load_cases.distribution: point load centered on the decking unit positioned 6 ft (1829 mm) from the west end; applied at the level of the decking / top of the north side stringer
load_cases.accompanying_weight_note: approximately 75 lb of dead weight placed on the decking above the opposite (south side) stringer to prevent lift-off (SSBC 11.4 Lateral Load Test of the Back Span)

load_cases.name: lateral_cantilever_sway
load_cases.distribution: point load applied 1 in (25.4 mm) from the east end of the south side stringer at its top
load_cases.rulebook_ref: SSBC 11.4 Lateral Load Test of the Cantilever

load_cases.name: buckling_under_vertical
load_cases.reference_state: V-B and V-C applied simultaneously (aggregate vertical condition)
load_cases.rulebook_ref: designer-imposed; not explicitly required by SSBC rules
load_cases.rationale: guards against compression-flange snap-through during the ~2 min live-load placement sequence

boundary_conditions.west_end_supports: both stringers supported at the west end inside the designated footings; model as a pinned reaction at one stringer end (Ux=Uy=Uz=0) and a roller at the opposite stringer end (Uy=Uz=0, Ux free) to respect SSBC rule 9.3.3 no-constraint-against-longitudinal-thermal-growth idealization
boundary_conditions.loose_bolt_connections: tied or coupled pair at each loose-bolt location; bolt stiffness equivalent to the team's selected commercial bolt in single or double shear; no pretension (loose bolts are snug-installed by hand during timed construction -- SSBC 8.2.3 does not require torque control)

objective.scoring_formula_reference:
SSBC rule 6 Structural Cost Cs:
  if measured_weight <= 150 lb: Cs = (total_weight - measured_weight) * 5000 $/lb + aggregate_deflection_in * 3150000 $/in
  elif 150 < measured_weight <= 300 lb: Cs = (measured_weight - 150) * 8333 $/lb + (total_weight - measured_weight) * 5000 $/lb + aggregate_deflection_in * 3150000 $/in
  else: Cs = (measured_weight - 221) * 16000 $/lb + (total_weight - measured_weight) * 5000 $/lb + aggregate_deflection_in * 3150000 $/in
objective.design_heuristic: target measured weight under 150 lb to stay in the lowest Cs bracket

output_format.body_count: multi-body (one body per fabricated member + one body per loose bolt + one body per loose nut)

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: SSBC 11.5.3 penalty trigger: if aggregate deflection > 2 in, +$4,000,000 Cc and +$10,000,000 Cs
SSBC 11.2.3.9 and 11.5.3 stop condition: loading ceases and bridge is disqualified
SSBC 8.2.2.2 inspection box (3 ft 6 in x 6 in x 4 in inner dimensions)
pass_fail_criteria.severity: dimensional-violation weight penalty per SSBC 9.3.1.1 (20 / 100 / 200 / 400 lb bands)

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: SSBC 9.2.1 (exactly 2 contiguous stringers) + 9.3.6 (each stringer 20-21 ft long) + 9.3.9 (adjacent-member horizontal separation <= 1/4 in, slope change small)
SSBC 6 Structural Cost Cs has step-function penalty above 150 lb and another at 300 lb; competitive bridges stay below 150 lb
AISC Steel Construction Manual Part 7 for team-selected bolt grade, shear and bearing capacity
designer-imposed; guards against snap-through during the ~2 min live-load sequence

element_types.members: beam B31 (or equivalent Timoshenko beam) for most members; shell S4R for members containing bolt-hole stress concentrations that govern the Cs score
element_types.connections: tied or coupled pair at each loose-bolt location with equivalent axial and shear stiffness

recommended_fea_setup.mesh_size_hint: beam element length <= 1/10 of member length; shell mesh size <= 10 mm in bolt-hole regions
analysis_types: linear static x 4 (V-B, V-C, L-B, L-C)
linear static x 1 (V-B + V-C combined for aggregate deflection)
linear buckling eigenvalue x 1 (under V-B + V-C)

interfaces.mediated_by: C-family bolt (at least one per faying surface)
interfaces.load_path: axial tension/compression and moment along the stringer centerline
interfaces.rule_reference: SSBC 9.4.1 (connection required), 9.4.2 (connection = at least one loose bolt through a faying surface), 9.3.9 (adjacent A-member separation <= 1/4 in)

interfaces.load_path: vertical shear and lateral force from deck/brace into stringer

between: A-family end member at west end
interfaces.mediated_by: pinned or roller reaction per simply-supported idealization

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: SSBC rule 8.2.2.2 forces every fabricated member inside a 42 x 6 x 4 in box. A 20 ft stringer therefore cannot be a single member -- the bridge is physically required to be an assembly of many short bolted pieces. This is the cleanest example in the competition catalog of a benchmark that collapses to nonsense when modeled monolithically.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: no wind or fluid scope at this span / load

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: fatigue evaluation (not applicable for single-load competition)
corrosion / galvanic compatibility between steel grades
aesthetics (SSBC rule 6 Aesthetics; separately scored, not FEA-relevant)
notes.known_deviations_from_2025_2026_rulebook: The 2025 and 2026 official rulebooks are behind AISC paywalls and could not be downloaded by the tooling that generated this sample. The 2022 public rulebook was used as the source of all numeric values. The SSBC clarifications page indicates that the structural rules -- stringer rule 9.2.1, stringer length 9.3.6, member box 8.2.2.2, vertical load 1600 + 750 lb with 100 + 50 lb preloads, aggregate-deflection $4M+$10M penalty at 2 in, and lateral 50 lb tests -- are stable year-over-year. The Cs formula coefficients (5000, 8333, 16000 $/lb; 3150000 $/in) may have been recalibrated in 2025 or 2026; consult the current spreadsheet before using the scoring formula for real evaluation.

eval_coverage: multi-part assembly where multi-part is forced by the rulebook (member box), not just a modeling choice
asymmetric loading (backspan vs cantilever) that produces opposite-signed D1 and D2 in some designs
mixed-element FEA (beam for stringers, shell at bolt-hole concentrations)
linear buckling eigenvalue on a fully assembled bolted structure
two independent lateral sway tests with lift-off-restraint deadweight
rulebook compliance checks expressed as first-class pass/fail items (R3, R4, R5)
scored objective (Cs = lightness + stiffness weighted) on top of hard pass/fail

rulebook_citations.8.2.2: Parts of a member are welded together; a member shall retain its shape.
rulebook_citations.8.2.2.2: All members shall fit into a right rectangular prism of inner dimensions 3 ft 6 in x 6 in x 4 in.
rulebook_citations.8.2.3.1: Loose bolts shall not have parts that flex or move.
rulebook_citations.8.2.3.2: Nominal length of loose bolts shall not exceed 3 in.
rulebook_citations.8.2.4.1: Nuts shall have the external shape of a hexagonal prism.
rulebook_citations.8.2.4.2: Nuts shall have internal threads that extend for the full circumference over the full length.
rulebook_citations.9.2.1: The bridge shall have exactly two stringers, each of which is contiguous.
rulebook_citations.9.3.1.1: Dimensional weight penalties: 20 lb for <=1/4 in violation; 100 lb for 1/4-1 in; 200 lb for 1-2 in; 400 lb for 2-3 in.
rulebook_citations.9.3.6: Each stringer shall be at minimum 20 ft long and at maximum 21 ft long.
rulebook_citations.9.3.7: The bridge shall not be wider than 3 ft 7 in at any location along the span.
rulebook_citations.9.3.9: Tops of stringers shall be free of holes, splits, separations, protrusions; between adjacent members a horizontal separation not exceeding 1/4 in is permitted.
rulebook_citations.9.4.1: All locations where one member touches another member require a connection.
rulebook_citations.9.4.2: Connection = at least one loose bolt through a faying surface; cam locks, dovetails, tube-in-tube connections prohibited.
rulebook_citations.9.4.3: Each individual hole in a member for a loose bolt shall be completely surrounded by the member; outer-ply holes small enough that the nut or bolt head cannot pass through.
rulebook_citations.10.4.2: Magnetic inspection: 25 lb penalty per non-compliant nut or loose bolt, 35 lb per non-compliant member.
rulebook_citations.11.2.3.9: If any deflection exceeds 2.5 in, loading shall cease and load shall be removed.
rulebook_citations.11.4: Lateral load tests: 50 lb lateral at 6 ft from west end (backspan) and at east end (cantilever); ~75 lb opposite-side dead weight.
rulebook_citations.11.5.1.1: Decking units are 3 ft long in the span direction; placed at position L (backspan) and 1 in from east end (cantilever).
rulebook_citations.11.5.3: Vertical load sequence: 100 lb preload backspan + 50 lb preload cantilever; zero D1, D2; 1600 lb live on backspan; 750 lb live on cantilever. Aggregate deflection > 2 in -> +$4M Cc and +$10M Cs.
rulebook_citations.6_structural_cost: Cs piecewise formula: 5000 $/lb for (total - measured) weight; 8333 $/lb for measured weight in (150, 300] lb bracket; 16000 $/lb for measured weight above 300 lb (baseline 221); 3150000 $/in for aggregate deflection.

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.
